Genera un canvas que copie exactamente el formato de este website https://resume.haveloc.com/sample_resume hazle scraping si quieres pero lo entregas en español y con import para pdf

Quiero tener mi cv ATS elegante pero minimalista

**Initiating Design Analysis**

I'm starting the design process by diving deep into the request. My first goal is to dissect the sample resume's structure and layout. I need to figure out which React components will replicate the different sections. I'm focusing on identifying the core elements that define the visual style of the target resume.


**Formulating Technical Specs**

I'm now nailing down the technical specifications. The resume must be Spanish-language, exportable as a PDF, and designed to be ATS-friendly. The tech stack will be React 18+, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. While I'm considering using Gemini API for future AI-powered enhancements, it's not strictly necessary for this static resume builder, though it could add powerful "Improve this section" features. Visually, the resume will have an elegant, minimalist aesthetic, with a single-column layout and clean typography.


**Refining Data Structures**

I've been working on the data structure for the resume. I'm focusing on organizing the information logically, with defined TypeScript interfaces for Profile, Education, Experience, Projects, and Skills. This will make it easier to manage the data and keep the code clean. I'm also planning the core React components: ResumePreview, Editor, PDFExporter, and a future GeminiEnhancer.


**Integrating Gemini API Functionality**

I've been planning the user interface. I'll use Tailwind for the "clean, serif/sans-serif" look, aiming for an A4 aspect ratio. The editor will be a clean form for data input.  For PDF export, the native browser's `window.print()` with a specifically styled `@media print` block should work great and retain ATS-friendliness.


**Building the Core Components**

I'm making progress building the core components. I've defined the data structures with TypeScript interfaces and designed the `ResumeCanvas`, `Editor`, and `App` components. I've also implemented the `improveText` function for the Gemini API. My approach focuses on using Tailwind CSS for a clean, user-friendly interface. Now, I'm working to integrate print styles into the `ResumeCanvas` to ensure the PDF export retains ATS-friendliness, while also making sure it looks amazing.


**Revising PDF Export**

I've refocused on the PDF export, prioritizing "export to PDF" as the core functionality, not "import." To aid this, I'm adding JSON import/export for saving progress. Now, I'm working on the design to make sure the `@media print` styles will hide the editor and perfectly render the resume. I'm also planning the core structure: `header`, `section` and `ul/li` for bullets. Finally, I'm going to add a "Magic" button in the Editor to use Gemini API to polish the Spanish text.


**Refining PDF Output**

I'm now revising my PDF export strategy. I'll use `createRoot` for rendering and have chosen Gemini 2.5 Flash for text improvements. I'm focusing on getting the XML format right, specifically for "Haveloc" style resumes. The goal is to provide a clean PDF, built around `window.print()` with `@media print` styles. My recent update will add import/export JSON for saving the user's progress. "Haveloc" has been added.


**Updating Visual Design**

I'm now focusing on refining the visual design of the PDF output. I'm incorporating Times New Roman or Garamond for a classic serif look, aiming for small caps or uppercase, bold headers with bottom borders. I'm also working to achieve a dense, high-information-density layout within the established "Haveloc" style framework. This new focus should significantly enhance the visual presentation of the exported resume.

Here is the complete code to generate a minimalist, ATS-friendly resume builder in Spanish, mimicking the "Haveloc" style. It includes a Gemini-powered "AI Writer" to improve resume content and a native PDF export feature.
